heading: Certain lease bidding arrangements prohibited
label: 6213 Certain lease bidding arrangements prohibited
notes: Amendments1978—Subsec. (c). substituted “in his discretion, consider a request from any person described in subsection (a) of this section for an exemption from the prohibition of this section” for “by amendment to the rule, exempt bidding for leases for lands located in frontier or other areas determined by the Secretary to be extremely high risk lands or to present unusually high cost exploration, or development, problems” in existing provisions and inserted provisions setting out the requisite finding of the Secretary and making arbitrariness and capriciousness of the Secretary’s findings the only bases for invalidation of those findings.Pub. children.heading: Promulgation of rule by Secretary of the Interior
children.label: a Promulgation of rule by Secretary of the Interior
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior shall, not later than 30 days after , prescribe and make effective a rule which prohibits the bidding for any right to develop crude oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids on any lands located on the Outer Continental Shelf by any person if more than one major oil company, more than one affiliate of a major oil company, or a major oil company and any affiliate of a major oil company, has or have a significant ownership interest in such person. Such rule shall define affiliate relationships and significant ownership interests.December 22, 1975

The term “major oil company” means any person who, individually or together with any other person with respect to which such person has an affiliate relationship or significant ownership interest, produced during a prior 6–month period specified by the Secretary, an average daily volume of 1,600,000 barrels of crude oil, natural gas liquids equivalents, and natural gas equivalents.
One barrel of natural gas equivalent equals 5,626 cubic feet of natural gas measured at 14.73 pounds per square inch (MSL) and 60 degrees Fahrenheit.
One barrel of natural gas liquids equivalent equals 1.454 barrels of natural gas liquids at 60 degrees Fahrenheit.
such lands have extremely high cost exploration or development problems; and
exploration and development will not occur on such lands unless such exemption is granted.

children.heading: Unitization of producing fields
children.label: d Unitization of producing fields
children.text: This section shall not be construed to prohibit the unitization of producing fields to increase production or maximize ultimate recovery of oil or natural gas, or both.

children.heading: Report to Congress covering extension of restrictions on joint bidding
children.label: e Report to Congress covering extension of restrictions on joint bidding

children.text: bidding for any right to develop crude oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids on Federal lands other than those located on the Outer Continental Shelf; and
bidding for any right to develop coal and oil shale on such lands.
